from pyspark.sql import functions as F

EVENT_DIR = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-EXTRACT/LLAttr/Event_95506"

events_raw = (
    spark.read.format("csv")
    .option("header", "true")
    .option("sep", "|")           # <-- VERY important
    .option("multiLine", "true")
    .option("escape", "\"")
    .load(EVENT_DIR)
)

print("Row count:", events_raw.count())
print("Columns:", events_raw.columns)
display(events_raw.limit(10))

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

# -----------------------------
# 1) Load Cases.csv -> cases_map(legacyCaseId, caseId)
# -----------------------------
cases_path = f"{mpesc_base_path}/config/Cases/Cases.csv"

cases_raw = (
    spark.read.format("csv")
    .option("header", "true")
    .option("multiLine", "true")
    .option("escape", "\"")
    .load(cases_path)
)

print("cases_raw rows:", cases_raw.count())
print("cases_raw columns:", cases_raw.columns)
display(cases_raw.limit(10))

# Trim column names
cols = [c.strip() for c in cases_raw.columns]
cases_raw2 = cases_raw.toDF(*cols)

# Detect column names
def norm(c): 
    return c.strip().lower().replace(" ", "").replace("_", "")

legacy_col = next((c for c in cases_raw2.columns if norm(c) in ["legacycaseid", "caseid"]), None)
guid_col   = next((c for c in cases_raw2.columns if norm(c) in ["guid", "caseguid", "caseidguid", "cboscaseid"]), None)

# Your CSV example earlier was "Case ID" + "GUID"
if legacy_col is None and "Case ID" in cases_raw2.columns:
    legacy_col = "Case ID"
if guid_col is None and "GUID" in cases_raw2.columns:
    guid_col = "GUID"

if legacy_col is None or guid_col is None:
    raise Exception(f"Could not identify columns for legacyCaseId & GUID. Found columns: {cases_raw2.columns}")

cases_map = (
    cases_raw2
    .select(
        F.trim(F.col(legacy_col).cast("string")).alias("legacyCaseId"),
        F.trim(F.col(guid_col).cast("string")).alias("caseId")
    )
    .filter(F.col("legacyCaseId").isNotNull() & (F.col("legacyCaseId") != ""))
    .filter(F.col("caseId").isNotNull() & (F.col("caseId") != ""))
    .dropDuplicates(["legacyCaseId"])
)

print("cases_map rows (non-null):", cases_map.count())
display(cases_map.orderBy("legacyCaseId").limit(20))


# -----------------------------
# 2) Key events to cases correctly
# -----------------------------
# In LLAttr exports, the case link is almost always ParentKeyID (not ID).
# We'll use ParentKeyID if it exists; otherwise fall back to ID with a loud warning.
event_case_key = "ParentKeyID" if "ParentKeyID" in events_raw.columns else "ID"
if event_case_key != "ParentKeyID":
    print("⚠️ WARNING: ParentKeyID not found in events_raw. Falling back to ID; mapping may be wrong.")

events_keyed = (
    events_raw
    .withColumn("legacyCaseId", F.trim(F.col(event_case_key).cast("string")))
    .withColumn("eventRowId", F.col("ID").cast("string") if "ID" in events_raw.columns else F.lit(None).cast("string"))
)

joined = events_keyed.join(cases_map, on="legacyCaseId", how="left")

# -----------------------------
# 3) Mapping coverage checks
# -----------------------------
total = joined.count()
mapped = joined.filter(F.col("caseId").isNotNull()).count()
unmapped = total - mapped

print("events total:", total)
print("mapped (have case GUID):", mapped)
print("unmapped:", unmapped)

# Helpful debug: show top unmapped legacyCaseIds
display(
    joined.filter(F.col("caseId").isNull())
          .select("legacyCaseId")
          .groupBy("legacyCaseId").count()
          .orderBy(F.desc("count"))
          .limit(30)
)

# -----------------------------
# 4) Safe preview of likely columns (no magic "25"/"26")
# -----------------------------
preview_cols = ["legacyCaseId", "caseId", "eventRowId"]

# add common LLAttr columns if present
for c in ["AttrID", "ValStr", "ValLong", "ValInt", "ValDate", "ValReal"]:
    if c in joined.columns:
        preview_cols.append(c)

display(joined.select(*preview_cols).limit(30))

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

def clean_str(col):
    c = col.cast("string")
    return (
        F.when(c.isNull(), F.lit(None).cast("string"))
         .when(F.trim(c) == "", F.lit(None).cast("string"))
         .when(F.lower(F.trim(c)).isin("undefined", "null", "none"), F.lit(None).cast("string"))
         .otherwise(F.trim(c))
    )

mapped_only = joined.filter(F.col("caseId").isNotNull())

print("mapped_only rows:", mapped_only.count())

# Debug preview (safe)
display(
    mapped_only.select(
        "legacyCaseId",
        "caseId",
        F.col("25").alias("typeOfEvent"),
        F.col("26").alias("status")
    ).limit(20)
)

# Use eventRowId if you created it earlier, otherwise fallback to ID
event_id_col = "eventRowId" if "eventRowId" in mapped_only.columns else "ID"

events_clean = (
    mapped_only
    .select(
        F.col("legacyCaseId").cast("string"),
        F.col("caseId").cast("string"),
        F.col(event_id_col).cast("string").alias("eventId"),

        clean_str(F.col("24")).alias("eventName"),
        clean_str(F.col("25")).alias("typeOfEvent_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("26")).alias("chartStatus_raw"),

        clean_str(F.col("30")).alias("venueLine1"),
        clean_str(F.col("31")).alias("venueLine2"),
        clean_str(F.col("32")).alias("venueTown"),
        clean_str(F.col("33")).alias("venuePostcode"),
        clean_str(F.col("42")).alias("venueCounty"),

        clean_str(F.col("35")).alias("startDate_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("36")).alias("startTime_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("37")).alias("endDate_raw"),

        clean_str(F.col("43")).alias("estPrepTime_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("44")).alias("estSitTime_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("45")).alias("estRepTime_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("47")).alias("dateEventRequested_raw"),
        clean_str(F.col("48")).alias("actualDuration_raw"),
    )
)

print("events_clean rows:", events_clean.count())
print("events_clean columns:", events_clean.columns)

display(events_clean.limit(100))

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

def combine_date_time(date_ts_col, time_str_col):
    """
    Combine a date timestamp with a time string if the time is parseable.
    Supports:
      - HH:mm / H:mm
      - HH.mm / H.mm  (converted to :)
      - HH or H       (assumes :00)
    Ignores non-times like AM/PM/USV and returns date as-is (midnight).
    """
    # normalise time string
    t = F.lower(F.trim(time_str_col.cast("string")))
    t = F.regexp_replace(t, r"\.", ":")  # 10.00 -> 10:00

    # treat empty / undefined / null as not usable
    t = F.when(
        t.isNull() | (t == "") | (t == "undefined") | (t == "null") | (t == "none"),
        F.lit(None).cast("string")
    ).otherwise(t)

    # Patterns:
    is_hhmm = t.rlike(r"^\d{1,2}:\d{2}$")   # 9:00, 10:15, 0:00
    is_hh   = t.rlike(r"^\d{1,2}$")         # 9, 10, 0

    # Build HH and MM safely
    hh_from_hhmm = F.lpad(F.split(t, ":").getItem(0), 2, "0")
    mm_from_hhmm = F.split(t, ":").getItem(1)

    hh_from_hh = F.lpad(t, 2, "0")
    mm_from_hh = F.lit("00")

    hh = F.when(is_hhmm, hh_from_hhmm).when(is_hh, hh_from_hh).otherwise(F.lit(None))
    mm = F.when(is_hhmm, mm_from_hhmm).when(is_hh, mm_from_hh).otherwise(F.lit(None))

    # combine into timestamp
    combined = F.to_timestamp(
        F.concat(
            F.date_format(date_ts_col, "yyyy-MM-dd"),
            F.lit(" "),
            hh, F.lit(":"), mm, F.lit(":00")
        )
    )

    return (
        F.when(date_ts_col.isNull(), F.lit(None).cast("timestamp"))
         .when(hh.isNotNull() & mm.isNotNull(), combined)
         .otherwise(date_ts_col)
    )

events_dt = (
    events_clean
    # your raw date strings look like "2015-01-14 00:00:00"
    .withColumn("startDate_ts", F.to_timestamp("startDate_raw"))
    .withColumn("endDate_ts", F.to_timestamp("endDate_raw"))
    # if you still have dateEventRequested_raw in your events_clean, keep it:
    .withColumn(
        "dateEventRequested_ts",
        F.to_timestamp("dateEventRequested_raw") if "dateEventRequested_raw" in events_clean.columns else F.lit(None).cast("timestamp")
    )
    .withColumn("confirmedStartDateTime", combine_date_time(F.col("startDate_ts"), F.col("startTime_raw")))
)

display(
    events_dt.select(
        "legacyCaseId",
        "typeOfEvent_raw",
        "chartStatus_raw",
        "startDate_raw",
        "startTime_raw",
        "confirmedStartDateTime",
        "endDate_raw",
        "endDate_ts"
    ).limit(20)
)

# Optional: see which time strings are "weird" / not parsed
display(
    events_dt
    .select("startTime_raw")
    .groupBy("startTime_raw").count()
    .orderBy(F.desc("count"))
    .limit(50)

from pyspark.sql import functions as F, types as T
import uuid

NAMESPACE_UUID = uuid.UUID("6ba7b810-9dad-11d1-80b4-00c04fd430c8")

@F.udf(returnType=T.StringType())
def uuid5_from_text(txt: str):
    if txt is None:
        return None
    s = txt.strip().lower()
    if s == "":
        return None
    return str(uuid.uuid5(NAMESPACE_UUID, s))

def pick_col(df, *candidates):
    """Return the first candidate column name that exists in df.columns."""
    for c in candidates:
        if c in df.columns:
            return c
    return None

# ---- resolve actual column names ----
line1_col = pick_col(events_dt, "venueLine1")
line2_col = pick_col(events_dt, "venueLine2")
town_col  = pick_col(events_dt, "venueTown", "venueTown_raw")
county_col = pick_col(events_dt, "venueCounty", "venueRegion_raw")  # col 42 is inconsistent; treat as region/county raw
pc_col    = pick_col(events_dt, "venuePostcode")

missing = [name for name, col in {
    "venueLine1": line1_col,
    "venueLine2": line2_col,
    "venueTown": town_col,
    "venueCountyOrRegion": county_col,
    "venuePostcode": pc_col,
}.items() if col is None]

if missing:
    raise Exception(f"Missing expected venue columns in events_dt: {missing}. Available: {events_dt.columns}")

def norm_addr(c):
    # lower/trim; convert empty/undefined/null/none -> null
    s = F.lower(F.trim(F.col(c).cast("string")))
    return F.when(
        s.isNull() | (s == "") | s.isin("undefined", "null", "none"),
        F.lit(None).cast("string")
    ).otherwise(s)

# Build stable key for the venue address
events_with_addrkey = (
    events_dt
    .withColumn("line1_n", norm_addr(line1_col))
    .withColumn("line2_n", norm_addr(line2_col))
    .withColumn("town_n",  norm_addr(town_col))
    .withColumn("county_n",norm_addr(county_col))
    .withColumn("pc_n",    norm_addr(pc_col))
    .withColumn(
        "addrKey",
        F.concat_ws("|",
            F.coalesce(F.col("line1_n"), F.lit("")),
            F.coalesce(F.col("line2_n"), F.lit("")),
            F.coalesce(F.col("town_n"),  F.lit("")),
            F.coalesce(F.col("county_n"),F.lit("")),
            F.coalesce(F.col("pc_n"),    F.lit("")),
        )
    )
    # hasVenue = at least one meaningful component exists
    .withColumn(
        "hasVenue",
        F.col("line1_n").isNotNull() | F.col("town_n").isNotNull() | F.col("pc_n").isNotNull()
        | F.col("line2_n").isNotNull() | F.col("county_n").isNotNull()
    )
    .withColumn("venueId", F.when(F.col("hasVenue"), uuid5_from_text(F.col("addrKey"))))
)

# Address rows to load (deduped)
venue_address_ready = (
    events_with_addrkey
    .filter(F.col("venueId").isNotNull())
    .select(
        F.col("venueId").alias("id"),
        # Use original (non-normalised) fields for output, but trim and clean
        F.col(line1_col).cast("string").alias("line1"),
        F.col(line2_col).cast("string").alias("line2"),
        F.col(town_col).cast("string").alias("townCity"),
        F.col(county_col).cast("string").alias("county"),
        F.col(pc_col).cast("string").alias("postcode"),
        F.lit(None).cast("string").alias("legacyCaseId")  # keep null unless you want case linkage
    )
    .dropDuplicates(["id"])
)

print("Unique venue addresses:", venue_address_ready.count())
display(venue_address_ready.limit(20))

# Events with venueId attached
events_with_venue = (
    events_with_addrkey
    .drop("addrKey")
    .drop("line1_n","line2_n","town_n","county_n","pc_n")
)

display(
    events_with_venue.select(
        "legacyCaseId", "caseId",
        "typeOfEvent_raw", "chartStatus_raw",
        "venueId",
        F.col(line1_col).alias("venueLine1"),
        F.col(town_col).alias("venueTown"),
        F.col(pc_col).alias("venuePostcode")
    ).limit(20)

type_lc   = F.lower(F.col("typeOfEvent_raw"))
status_lc = F.lower(F.col("chartStatus_raw"))

events_mapped = (
    events_with_venue
    .withColumn(
        "procedureTypeId",
        F.when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bhearing\b"), "hearing")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\binquiry\b"), "inquiry")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bpre[-\s]?inquiry\b"), "inquiry")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bsite\s*visit\b"), "site-visit")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\badvisory\s*visit\b"), "site-visit")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bwritten\b"), "written-reps")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bin[-\s]?house\b"), "admin-in-house")
         .otherwise(None)
    )
    .withColumn(
        "procedureStatusId",
        F.when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bwithdraw"), "withdrawn")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bfallen"), "fallen-away")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bcancel"), "cancelled")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bchange\s+of\s+procedure"), "changed-procedure-type")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bcomplete"), "completed")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bconfirm"), "active")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bnew\b"), "active")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bresched"), "active")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\boffer"), "active")
         .when(status_lc.rlike(r"\bpostpon"), "active")
         .otherwise(None)
    )
    .withColumn(
        "procedureArrangementId",
        F.when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bvirtual\b"), "virtual")
         .when(type_lc.rlike(r"\bsite\s*visit\b"), "face-to-face")
         .otherwise(None)
    )
)
display(events_mapped)

from pyspark.sql import functions as F

def nullify_str(col):
    s = F.trim(col.cast("string"))
    return (
        F.when(col.isNull(), None)
         .when(F.lower(s).isin("undefined", "null", "none", ""), None)
         .otherwise(s)
    )

def combine_date_time(date_ts_col, time_str_col):
    """
    Combine date + time into a timestamp using Horizon time formats.

    Handles:
      - HH:mm / H:mm
      - HH.mm / H.mm
      - HHmm / HMM / 4-digit (e.g. 1100, 0930, 900)
      - '10am' / '10pm' (and variants with spaces)
    Ignores tokens like AM/PM/USV and falls back to date (midnight).
    """
    # normalise
    t = nullify_str(time_str_col)
    t = F.lower(t)
    t = F.regexp_replace(t, r"\s+", "")   # remove spaces
    t = F.regexp_replace(t, r"\.", ":")   # 10.00 -> 10:00

    # reject non-times
    t = F.when(t.isin("am", "pm", "a.m", "p.m", "a.m.", "p.m.", "usv"), None).otherwise(t)

    # convert HHmm / HMM / 4-digit -> HH:MM
    is_digits = t.rlike(r"^\d{3,4}$")
    hh_d = F.when(F.length(t) == 3, F.lpad(F.substring(t, 1, 1), 2, "0")) \
            .otherwise(F.substring(t, 1, 2))
    mm_d = F.when(F.length(t) == 3, F.substring(t, 2, 2)) \
            .otherwise(F.substring(t, 3, 2))
    t = F.when(is_digits, F.concat(hh_d, F.lit(":"), mm_d)).otherwise(t)

    # convert 10am / 10pm -> HH:00 (24h)
    is_am = t.rlike(r"^\d{1,2}am$")
    is_pm = t.rlike(r"^\d{1,2}pm$")
    hh = F.regexp_extract(t, r"^(\d{1,2})", 1).cast("int")

    hh_24 = (
        F.when(is_am & (hh == 12), F.lit(0))
         .when(is_am, hh)
         .when(is_pm & (hh == 12), F.lit(12))
         .when(is_pm, hh + 12)
    )

    t = F.when(is_am | is_pm, F.concat(F.lpad(hh_24.cast("string"), 2, "0"), F.lit(":00"))).otherwise(t)

    # final accept: H:MM or HH:MM
    is_hhmm = t.rlike(r"^\d{1,2}:\d{2}$")
    hh_f = F.lpad(F.split(t, ":").getItem(0), 2, "0")
    mm_f = F.split(t, ":").getItem(1)

    combined = F.to_timestamp(
        F.concat(F.date_format(date_ts_col, "yyyy-MM-dd"), F.lit(" "), hh_f, F.lit(":"), mm_f, F.lit(":00"))
    )

    return (
        F.when(date_ts_col.isNull(), F.lit(None).cast("timestamp"))
         .when(is_hhmm, combined)
         .otherwise(date_ts_col)  # fallback to midnight if time unusable
    )

events_dt = (
    events_clean
    .withColumn("startDate_raw", nullify_str(F.col("startDate_raw")))
    .withColumn("endDate_raw", nullify_str(F.col("endDate_raw")))
    .withColumn("dateEventRequested_raw", nullify_str(F.col("dateEventRequested_raw")))
    .withColumn("startTime_raw", nullify_str(F.col("startTime_raw")))
    .withColumn("startDate_ts", F.to_timestamp("startDate_raw"))
    .withColumn("endDate_ts", F.to_timestamp("endDate_raw"))
    .withColumn("dateEventRequested_ts", F.to_timestamp("dateEventRequested_raw"))
    .withColumn("confirmedStartDateTime", combine_date_time(F.col("startDate_ts"), F.col("startTime_raw")))
)

display(
    events_dt.select(
        "legacyCaseId",
        "typeOfEvent_raw",
        "chartStatus_raw",
        "startDate_raw",
        "startTime_raw",
        "confirmedStartDateTime",
        "endDate_raw",
        "endDate_ts"
    ).limit(20)

from pyspark.sql import functions as F, types as T

# ---- helpers ----
def nullify_str(col):
    s = F.trim(col.cast("string"))
    return (
        F.when(col.isNull(), None)
         .when(F.lower(s).isin("undefined", "null", "none", ""), None)
         .otherwise(s)
    )

def to_decimal_days(col):
    """
    Converts messy numeric strings like '.25', '1.', '00.00' to Decimal(10,2).
    Returns NULL for non-numeric things like '2 hours', '456', etc.
    """
    s = nullify_str(col)
    s = F.regexp_replace(s, r"^(\.)", "0.")       # .25 -> 0.25
    s = F.regexp_replace(s, r"(\.)$", "")         # 1. -> 1
    is_num = s.rlike(r"^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$")      # allow integers/decimals
    return F.when(is_num, s.cast("decimal(10,2)")).otherwise(F.lit(None).cast("decimal(10,2)"))

# shorthand
ptype = F.col("procedureTypeId")
start = F.col("confirmedStartDateTime").cast("timestamp")
end   = F.col("endDate_ts").cast("timestamp")
venue = F.col("venueId").cast("string")

procedure_ready = (
    events_mapped
    .select(
        # required keys
        F.col("caseId").cast("string").alias("caseId"),
        F.col("legacyCaseId").cast("string").alias("legacyCaseId"),
        F.col("eventId").cast("string").alias("legacyEventId"),

        # procedure core
        F.col("procedureTypeId").cast("string").alias("procedureTypeId"),
        F.col("procedureStatusId").cast("string").alias("procedureStatusId"),
        F.col("procedureArrangementId").cast("string").alias("procedureArrangementId"),

        # -------------------------
        # Dates (mapped from start/end)
        # -------------------------
        # Site visit
        F.when(ptype == "site-visit", start).otherwise(None).alias("siteVisitDate"),

        # Hearing timeline
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", start).otherwise(None).alias("confirmedHearingDate"),
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", end).otherwise(None).alias("hearingClosedDate"),

        # Inquiry timeline
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", start).otherwise(None).alias("confirmedInquiryDate"),
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", end).otherwise(None).alias("inquiryClosedDate"),

        # Pre-inquiry meeting (if you mapped pre-inquiry -> inquiry type, then this will be NULL)
        # If you want pre-inquiry preserved, we need a flag from typeOfEvent_raw; see note below.
        F.lit(None).cast("timestamp").alias("preInquiryMeetingDate"),

        # Admin in-house
        F.when(ptype == "admin-in-house", start).otherwise(None).alias("inHouseDate"),

        # Written reps offer date
        F.when(ptype == "written-reps", start).otherwise(None).alias("offerForWrittenRepresentationsDate"),

        # -------------------------
        # Durations (43/44/45/48)
        # -------------------------
        # Hearing duration fields
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", to_decimal_days(F.col("actualDuration_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("lengthOfHearingEvent"),
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", to_decimal_days(F.col("estPrepTime_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("hearingPreparationTimeDays"),
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", to_decimal_days(F.col("estSitTime_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("hearingSittingTimeDays"),
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", to_decimal_days(F.col("estRepTime_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("hearingReportingTimeDays"),
        F.lit(None).cast("decimal(10,2)").alias("hearingTravelTimeDays"),   # no confirmed source column yet
        F.lit(None).cast("boolean").alias("hearingInTarget"),              # no confirmed source column yet

        # Inquiry duration fields
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", to_decimal_days(F.col("actualDuration_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("lengthOfInquiryEvent"),
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", to_decimal_days(F.col("estPrepTime_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("inquiryPreparationTimeDays"),
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", to_decimal_days(F.col("estSitTime_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("inquirySittingTimeDays"),
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", to_decimal_days(F.col("estRepTime_raw"))).otherwise(None).alias("inquiryReportingTimeDays"),
        F.lit(None).cast("decimal(10,2)").alias("inquiryTravelTimeDays"),   # no confirmed source column yet
        F.lit(None).cast("boolean").alias("inquiryInTarget"),              # no confirmed source column yet

        # -------------------------
        # Venues (use Address table you already build)
        # -------------------------
        F.when(ptype == "hearing", venue).otherwise(None).alias("hearingVenueId"),
        F.when(ptype == "inquiry", venue).otherwise(None).alias("inquiryVenueId"),

        # Step (if you already set this elsewhere keep it; otherwise default ProcedureOne)
        F.lit("ProcedureOne").alias("step"),

        # debug
        F.col("typeOfEvent_raw"),
        F.col("chartStatus_raw"),
    )
)

# recommended filter
procedure_ready_final = procedure_ready.filter(F.col("procedureTypeId").isNotNull())

out_base = f"{mpesc_base_path}/MPESC-TRANSFORM/Procedure"

out_address_dir   = f"{out_base}/ProcedureVenues_Address"
out_procedure_dir = f"{out_base}/Procedure"

# Address venues -> MPESC-TRANSFORM ✅
venue_address_ready.coalesce(1) \
    .write.mode("overwrite") \
    .option("header", "true") \
    .csv(out_address_dir)

# Procedure -> MPESC-TRANSFORM ✅
procedure_ready.coalesce(1) \
    .write.mode("overwrite") \
    .option("header", "true") \
    .csv(out_procedure_dir)

print("Wrote Address to:", out_address_dir)
print("Wrote Procedure to:", out_procedure_dir)
